chapter_title.ar: باب اسْتِيمَارِ الْبِكْرِ وَ مَنْ يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ اسْتِيمَارُهَا وَ مَنْ لَا يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 56 – Permission of (marriage of) the virgin and the one Obligated upon permitting her, and the one on who there is no such Obligation

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/128)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/128)
text: لَا تَزَوَّجُ ذَوَاتُ الْآبَاءِ مِنَ الْأَبْكَارِ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ آبَائِهِنَّ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Ala’a Bin Razeyn, from Ibn Abu Yafour,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The virgins who are with a father cannot get married except by the permission of their fathers’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from al-‘Ala’ ibn Razin from ibn abu ibn abu Ya’fur who has said the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘A virgin woman who has her father must not marry without his permission.’”
